name: Vietnamese Noodle Soup
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons kosher salt (or less if using purchased stock)
1/4 cup chopped Thai or serrano chiles
1/2 cup chopped Thai basil (holy basil or sweet basil)
2 pounds wide rice stick noodles (soaked for 20 minutes in warm water and drained)
recipeInstructions:
This is an excellent, flavorful soup adapted from Barbara Kafka's book. If using homemade chicken stock, divide the cooked chicken among the soup bowls along with the noodles.
Tie star anise, peppercorns, and cloves in a cloth spice bag or piece of cheesecloth.
In a nonstick pan over high heat, arrange 2 unpeeled, quartered onions and ginger (cut-side-down). Cook until beginning to blacken, about 10 minutes. Remove ginger and cook the remaining onions for another 10 minutes before removing them from the pan. Rinse the onions and ginger.
Bring stock to a boil in a large soup pot. Add onions, ginger, spice bag, fish sauce, and sugar. Reduce heat and simmer for 40 minutes.
Strain broth and return liquid to the pot over low heat. Stir in salt, remaining peeled sliced onions, green onions, chiles, cilantro, and basil. Heat soup for another 15 minutes.
Bring a large pot of water to boil. Add soaked rice noodles and cook for 2 to 3 minutes. Drain noodles and divide among 6 large individual-serving soup bowls. Pour about 2 1/2 cups soup over noodles in each bowl. Garnish with mung bean sprouts and serve with lime wedges. Makes 6 generous servings.
Each serving using half the amount of salt contains approximately 7 bread/starch exchanges, 1/2 fat exchange; 535 calories, 108 grams carbohydrate, 16 grams protein, 3 grams fat (including 1 gram saturated fat), 1880 mg sodium, 110 mg calcium, and 5 grams dietary fiber.
